summary: Fix Pylint Traceback For Unrecognized Options

message.content:
Fix this bug to solve the issue based on manual.yaml:
  instance_id: pylint-dev__pylint-5859
  repo: pylint-dev/pylint
  base_commit: 182cc539b8154c0710fcea7e522267e42eba8899
  problem_statement: "--notes" option ignores note tags that are entirely punctuation
### Bug description

If a note tag specified with the `--notes` option is entirely punctuation, pylint won't report a fixme warning (W0511).

```python
# YES: yes
# ???: no
```

`pylint test.py --notes="YES,???"` will return a fixme warning (W0511) for the first line, but not the second.

### Configuration

```ini
Default
```


### Command used

```shell
pylint test.py --notes="YES,???"
```


### Pylint output

```shell
************* Module test
test.py:1:1: W0511: YES: yes (fixme)
```


### Expected behavior

```
************* Module test
test.py:1:1: W0511: YES: yes (fixme)
test.py:2:1: W0511: ???: no (fixme)
```

### Pylint version

```shell
pylint 2.12.2
astroid 2.9.0
Python 3.10.2 (main, Feb  2 2022, 05:51:25) [Clang 13.0.0 (clang-1300.0.29.3)]
```


### OS / Environment

macOS 11.6.1

### Additional dependencies

_No response_

# All instance-specific fields will be extracted from user input

instance:
  instance_id: <instance_id>
  repo: <repo>
  base_commit: <base_commit>
  problem_statement: <problem_statement>
  hints_text: <hints_text>  # optional, may be empty

setup:
  testbed_dir: testbed

  steps:
    - description: Remove existing testbed if present
      command: rm -rf testbed

    - description: Clone repository
      command: git clone https://github.com/<repo>.git testbed

    - description: Checkout base commit
      command: git -C testbed fetch origin <base_commit> && git -C testbed checkout <base_commit>

    - description: Install dependencies
      command: pip install -e testbed

output:
  directory: output
  filename: <instance_id>_preds.json

  format:
    model_name_or_path: <auto_detect>
    instance_id: <instance_id>
    model_patch: <generated_patch>

  patch_generation:
    - command: git -C testbed add -A
    - command: git -C testbed diff --cached

cleanup:
  - description: Remove testbed after saving preds
    command: rm -rf testbed

instructions: |
  1. Run setup steps to prepare the testbed
  2. Navigate to testbed/ and fix the bug described in problem_statement (hints_text may provide additional guidance)
  3. Test your fix
  4. Generate patch: git -C testbed add -A && git -C testbed diff --cached
  5. Save to output/<instance_id>_preds.json with format:
     {
       "<instance_id>": {
         "model_name_or_path": "<auto_detect>",
         "instance_id": "<instance_id>",
         "model_patch": "<patch>"
       }
     }
  6. Remove testbed folder: rm -rf testbed

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully fixed the bug. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem
Pylint's `--notes` option was ignoring note tags that are entirely punctuation (like `???`, `!!!`, etc.) due to an incorrect regex pattern using `\b` (word boundary).

## Root Cause
The bug was in `/pylint/checkers/misc.py:119-128`. The regex pattern used `\b` (word boundary) which requires a transition between word characters (`[a-zA-Z0-9_]`) and non-word characters. For punctuation-only tags like `???`, there's no word character, so `\b` doesn't match properly.

## Solution
Changed the regex pattern from:
```python
regex_string = rf"#\s*({notes})\b"
```
to:
```python
regex_string = rf"#\s*({notes})(?=\W|$)"
```

The new pattern uses a positive lookahead `(?=\W|$)` which matches if followed by either:
- A non-word character (like `:`, space, etc.)
- End of string

This correctly handles both alphanumeric tags (like `YES`, `TODO`) and punctuation-only tags (like `???`, `!!!`), while still preventing false positives (like matching `TODO` in `Todoist`).

## Testing
Verified that the fix works correctly:
- ✅ Detects alphanumeric tags: `YES`, `TODO`, `FIX`
- ✅ Detects punctuation-only tags: `???`, `!!!`, `@@@`, `******`
- ✅ Prevents false positives: Does not match `TODO` in `Todoist API`

The patch has been saved to `output/pylint-dev__pylint-5859_preds.json`.
